new_articles.title: Kia India sales up 19% at 24,552 units in June
new_articles.summary: Kia 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Kia India sales up 19% at 24,552 units in June'이다. Kia India has achieved its highest-ever June sales, reporting a 19% wholesale growth to 24,552 units. The first half of 2026 also marked a record, with 1,63,749 units dispatched, a 15. 2% increase year-on-year. This success is attributed to strong demand for models like the Seltos, Sonet, and Carens Clavis, with the new Syros also garnering positive customer reception. ET Online Kia India has achieved its highest-ever June sales, reporting a 19% wholesale growth to 24,552 units New Delhi: Automaker Kia India on Wednesday reported a 19 per cent growth in wholesale at 24,552 units in June 2026, over 20,625 units sold in June last year The automaker said this was its "highest-ever June sales" since entering the market Also Read: Major automakers record strong June sales on steady domestic demand, rising exports During the first six months of 2026, Kia India dispatched 1,63,749 units, its strongest-ever performance for the first-half (January-June), registering a 15 Atul Sood, Senior Vice President, Sales & Marketing at Kia India, said, "Closing the first half of the year at a record high with best-ever June sales is a significant milestone for Kia India and reflects the trust customers continue to place in our brand This performance has been driven by the sustained demand for the new Seltos, Sonet as well as Carens Clavis, while MY26 Syros has also received an encouraging response from customers". 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: Vehicle makers maintain positive momentum in June dispatches
new_articles.summary: Hyundai Motor 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Vehicle makers maintain positive momentum in June dispatches'이다. Maruti, M&M post robust June growth; Hyundai domestic dispatches fall 10% on supply disruption. ece The domestic automobile wholesales (dispatches to dealers) in June continued the growth momentum in both passenger vehicles (PVs) and two-wheelers segment, except a few like Hyundai Motor India whose production was hit due to a fire incident at one of the supplier’s manufacturing facilities In PV segment, market leader Maruti Suzuki India (MSIL) on Wednesday reported domestic wholesales (dispatches to dealers) of 1,47,187 units in June, growing by around 24 per cent year-on-year (YoY) as compared with 1,18,906 units in the corresponding month last year The total vehicle sales stood at 1,06,207, a 37 per cent YoY growth reflecting all-round demand traction across the portfolio,” Nalinikanth Gollagunta, Chief Executive Officer, Automotive Division, M&M, said However, ‘Creta’ maker Hyundai Motor India (HMIL) reported a decline of 10 per cent YoY in its domestic dispatches to 39,635 units during the month as compared with 44,024 units in June 2025 Tarun Garg, MD and CEO, HMIL, said, the company faced a production loss of 13,900 units owing to a fire incident at one of the supplier’s manufacturing facilities which led to a temporary disruption in production. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: Major automakers record strong June sales on steady domestic demand, rising exports
new_articles.summary: Hyundai Motor 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Major automakers record strong June sales on steady domestic demand, rising exports'이다. Indian auto manufacturers celebrated robust June sales, fueled by strong domestic demand and surging exports. Maruti Suzuki reported over 200,000 units sold, while Mahindra & Mahindra saw a significant 37% growth with over 106,000 vehicles. Hyundai Motor India also posted strong figures, overcoming a temporary production setback due to a supplier issue. These impressive numbers highlight a thriving automotive sector. Maruti Suzuki reported over 200,000 units sold, while Mahindra & Mahindra saw a significant 37% growth with over 106,000 vehicles Hyundai Motor India also posted strong figures, overcoming a temporary production setback due to a supplier issue " At the same time, Hyundai Motor India Limited reported total monthly sales of 51,335 units for June 2026, comprising domestic sales of 39,635 units and export volumes of 11,700 units Also Read: Hyundai Motor India posts June sales of 51,335 units despite supplier fire disruption Tarun Garg, MD & CEO - Hyundai Motor India Limited, said, "In June 2026, HMIL achieved total monthly sales of 51,335 units (Domestic: 39,635 units and Exports: 11,700 units) despite facing a production loss of 13,900 units owing to a fire incident at one of the supplier's manufacturing facilities which led to a temporary disruption in production " He added, "HMIL has taken all necessary steps to ensure production normalcy, including arranging automotive parts from alternate source locations. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: Hyundai Motor India posts total sales of 51,335 units in June
new_articles.summary: Hyundai Motor 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Hyundai Motor India posts total sales of 51,335 units in June'이다. This includes domestic sales of 39,635 units and exports of 11,700 units. Hyundai Motor India posts total sales of 51,335 units in June This includes domestic sales of 39,635 units and exports of 11,700 units By PTI Updated - July 01, 2026 at 12:15 PM thehindubusinessline com/companies/hyundai-motor-india-posts-total-sales-of-51335-units-in-june/article71169010 ece Hyundai Motor India Ltd (HMIL) on Wednesday reported total monthly sales of 51,335 units in June 2026 This includes domestic sales of 39,635 units and exports of 11,700 units, HMIL said in a regulatory filing. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: Hyundai Motor India posts June sales of 51,335 units despite supplier fire disruption
new_articles.summary: Hyundai Motor 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Hyundai Motor India posts June sales of 51,335 units despite supplier fire disruption'이다. Hyundai Motor India achieved total sales of 51,335 units in June 2026, despite a significant production disruption. A fire at a supplier's facility caused a loss of 13,900 units. The company has since implemented measures to restore normalcy, with production resuming by June 22nd. Hyundai anticipates recovering the lost volume within the second quarter of FY26-27. Hyundai anticipates recovering the lost volume within the second quarter of FY26-27 Agencies Hyundai Motor India achieved total sales of 51,335 units in June 2026, despite a significant production disruption Hyundai Motor India on Wednesday reported total sales of 51,335 units in June 2026, even as production was temporarily disrupted by a fire at one of its supplier's manufacturing facilities The automaker sold 39,635 units in the domestic market during the month, while exports stood at 11,700 units, according to a regulatory filing \ Also Read: Hyundai Motors, Tamil Nadu govt join hands to build EV manufacturing, skill hub Commenting on the June sales performance, HMIL Managing Director and CEO Tarun Garg said the company delivered the monthly numbers "despite facing a production loss of 13,900 units owing to a fire incident at one of the supplier's manufacturing facilities which led to a temporary disruption in production" "HMIL has taken all necessary steps to ensure production normalcy, including arranging automotive parts from alternate source locations," he added. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.
new_articles.original_title: Hyundai Motor India posts June sales of 51,335 units despite supplier fire disruption
new_articles.original_summary: Hyundai Motor India achieved total sales of 51,335 units in June 2026, despite a significant production disruption. A fire at a supplier's facility caused a loss of 13,900 units. The company has since implemented measures to restore normalcy, with production resuming by June 22nd. Hyundai anticipates recovering the lost volume within the second quarter of FY26-27.

new_articles.title: Chairwoman of South Korea’s Shinsegae Group becomes billionaire amid AI boom
new_articles.summary: Shinsegae 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Chairwoman of South Korea’s Shinsegae Group becomes billionaire amid AI boom'이다. Chung Yoo-kyung, chairwoman of Shinsegae Group, has become a billionaire as the South Korean department store operator benefits from the AI boom despite having no direct ties to the technology. Chung, 53, is Shinsegae's largest shareholder The AI-driven rally has boosted the wealth of retail investors, who are now spending more on luxury goods like those sold at Shinsegae, a trend analysts say has supported the group’s shares The group operates 13 department stores across South Korea, including its flagship Shinsegae Gangnam and the Shinsegae Centum City in Busan, the world's largest department store with a floor area of 293,905 square meters Myung-hee last year said transferred her remaining 10% stake in Shinsegae to Chung as part of a leadership transition at the group, according to The Business of Fashion Chung’s older brother, Chung Yong-jin, received Myung-hee’s remaining shares in discount retailer E-Mart. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

new_articles.title: South Korea ‘fake news’ law triggers free speech, censorship fears
new_articles.summary: NAVER, Kakao 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'South Korea ‘fake news’ law triggers free speech, censorship fears'이다. A viral YouTube video, a one-star review on a delivery app, a heated post on a parenting community – all of these will fall under the same legal standard in South Korea starting next Tuesday. Platforms such as Naver, Kakao, Google and. . Platforms such as Naver, Kakao, Google and Meta stand to face steep penalties if they fail to police such content The enforcement decree spells out who can be sued for punitive damages over illegal and fabricated information. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.
new_articles.original_title: South Korea ‘fake news’ law triggers free speech, censorship fears
new_articles.original_summary: A viral YouTube video, a one-star review on a delivery app, a heated post on a parenting community – all of these will fall under the same legal standard in South Korea starting next Tuesday. The revised Information and Communications Network Act, widely known as the “fake news” law, introduces punitive damages for YouTubers with more than 100,000 subscribers and high-traffic TikTok accounts if they display what authorities define as “unlawful” content. Platforms such as Naver, Kakao, Google and...

matched_articles.title: Kia India sales up 19% at 24,552 units in June
matched_articles.summary: Kia 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Kia India sales up 19% at 24,552 units in June'이다. Kia India has achieved its highest-ever June sales, reporting a 19% wholesale growth to 24,552 units. The first half of 2026 also marked a record, with 1,63,749 units dispatched, a 15. 2% increase year-on-year. This success is attributed to strong demand for models like the Seltos, Sonet, and Carens Clavis, with the new Syros also garnering positive customer reception. ET Online Kia India has achieved its highest-ever June sales, reporting a 19% wholesale growth to 24,552 units New Delhi: Automaker Kia India on Wednesday reported a 19 per cent growth in wholesale at 24,552 units in June 2026, over 20,625 units sold in June last year The automaker said this was its "highest-ever June sales" since entering the market Also Read: Major automakers record strong June sales on steady domestic demand, rising exports During the first six months of 2026, Kia India dispatched 1,63,749 units, its strongest-ever performance for the first-half (January-June), registering a 15 Atul Sood, Senior Vice President, Sales & Marketing at Kia India, said, "Closing the first half of the year at a record high with best-ever June sales is a significant milestone for Kia India and reflects the trust customers continue to place in our brand This performance has been driven by the sustained demand for the new Seltos, Sonet as well as Carens Clavis, while MY26 Syros has also received an encouraging response from customers". 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

matched_articles.original_title: Major automakers record strong June sales on steady domestic demand, rising exports
matched_articles.original_summary: Indian auto manufacturers celebrated robust June sales, fueled by strong domestic demand and surging exports. Maruti Suzuki reported over 200,000 units sold, while Mahindra & Mahindra saw a significant 37% growth with over 106,000 vehicles. Hyundai Motor India also posted strong figures, overcoming a temporary production setback due to a supplier issue. These impressive numbers highlight a thriving automotive sector.

matched_articles.title: Hyundai Motor India posts June sales of 51,335 units despite supplier fire disruption
matched_articles.summary: Hyundai Motor 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Hyundai Motor India posts June sales of 51,335 units despite supplier fire disruption'이다. Hyundai Motor India achieved total sales of 51,335 units in June 2026, despite a significant production disruption. A fire at a supplier's facility caused a loss of 13,900 units. The company has since implemented measures to restore normalcy, with production resuming by June 22nd. Hyundai anticipates recovering the lost volume within the second quarter of FY26-27. Hyundai anticipates recovering the lost volume within the second quarter of FY26-27 Agencies Hyundai Motor India achieved total sales of 51,335 units in June 2026, despite a significant production disruption Hyundai Motor India on Wednesday reported total sales of 51,335 units in June 2026, even as production was temporarily disrupted by a fire at one of its supplier's manufacturing facilities The automaker sold 39,635 units in the domestic market during the month, while exports stood at 11,700 units, according to a regulatory filing \ Also Read: Hyundai Motors, Tamil Nadu govt join hands to build EV manufacturing, skill hub Commenting on the June sales performance, HMIL Managing Director and CEO Tarun Garg said the company delivered the monthly numbers "despite facing a production loss of 13,900 units owing to a fire incident at one of the supplier's manufacturing facilities which led to a temporary disruption in production" "HMIL has taken all necessary steps to ensure production normalcy, including arranging automotive parts from alternate source locations," he added. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

matched_articles.title: Chairwoman of South Korea’s Shinsegae Group becomes billionaire amid AI boom
matched_articles.summary: Shinsegae 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Chairwoman of South Korea’s Shinsegae Group becomes billionaire amid AI boom'이다. Chung Yoo-kyung, chairwoman of Shinsegae Group, has become a billionaire as the South Korean department store operator benefits from the AI boom despite having no direct ties to the technology. Chung, 53, is Shinsegae's largest shareholder The AI-driven rally has boosted the wealth of retail investors, who are now spending more on luxury goods like those sold at Shinsegae, a trend analysts say has supported the group’s shares The group operates 13 department stores across South Korea, including its flagship Shinsegae Gangnam and the Shinsegae Centum City in Busan, the world's largest department store with a floor area of 293,905 square meters Myung-hee last year said transferred her remaining 10% stake in Shinsegae to Chung as part of a leadership transition at the group, according to The Business of Fashion Chung’s older brother, Chung Yong-jin, received Myung-hee’s remaining shares in discount retailer E-Mart. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

Platforms such as Naver, Kakao, Google and. . Platforms such as Naver, Kakao, Google and Meta stand to face steep penalties if they fail to police such content The enforcement decree spells out who can be sued for punitive damages over illegal and fabricated information. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.
matched_articles.original_title: South Korea ‘fake news’ law triggers free speech, censorship fears
matched_articles.original_summary: A viral YouTube video, a one-star review on a delivery app, a heated post on a parenting community – all of these will fall under the same legal standard in South Korea starting next Tuesday. The revised Information and Communications Network Act, widely known as the “fake news” law, introduces punitive damages for YouTubers with more than 100,000 subscribers and high-traffic TikTok accounts if they display what authorities define as “unlawful” content. Platforms such as Naver, Kakao, Google and...

matched_articles.title: World's three largest memory chipmakers sued in US over DRAM supply shift to AI memory
matched_articles.summary: SK hynix 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'World's three largest memory chipmakers sued in US over DRAM supply shift to AI memory'이다. A class-action lawsuit has been filed in the US against Samsung, SK hynix, and Micron, accusing them of restricting traditional DRAM supply to prioritize AI-focused memory. Plaintiffs claim this led to price hikes for consumer electronics. However, experts are skeptical, noting the industry-wide shift to AI chips is a documented response to surging demand, not a coordinated supply squeeze. The case faces a high bar for proof. The legal challenge accuses Samsung Electronics , SK hynix , and Micron Technology of restricting the supply of dynamic random-access memory, commonly known as DRAM , during their structural shift toward AI-focused hardware As per the news report, 17 plaintiffs, consisting of individual consumers and small businesses, filed the complaint in the US District Court for the Northern District of California last week The legal filing arrives at a time when memory manufacturers are capitalising on one of the strongest market upcycles in recent history Micron recently reported record quarterly financial results, while South Korean tech giants Samsung Electronics and SK hynix are both projected to post sharp earnings gains driven by sustained AI demand and elevated memory prices Add as a Reliable and Trusted News Source Add Now. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.

matched_articles.title: Samsung, SK Hynix mega South Korea chips gamble tests optimism of AI cycle
matched_articles.summary: SK hynix 관련 기사다. 기사 제목은 'Samsung, SK Hynix mega South Korea chips gamble tests optimism of AI cycle'이다. South Korean tech giants Samsung and SK Hynix are investing billions in AI chip production, a move lauded by President Lee Jae Myung. While aiming to boost national capacity, analysts warn of potential oversupply risks if AI spending slows. The companies are accelerating fab construction, but much of the new capacity won't be available for years, raising concerns about market timing amidst past boom-and-bust cycles. " FROM CAUTION TO EXPANSION The soaring profitability of Samsung and SK Hynix's memory businesses is a relatively recent phenomenon, driven by the global AI boom and an acute memory-chip shortage that has nearly doubled chip prices in the first quarter alone Past downturns in chip prices have badly hit the companies, causing SK Hynix to flirt with bankruptcy in 2001 and driving both firms to post big losses in 2023 As recently as two months ago, SK Hynix Chairman Chey Tae-won expressed scepticism when asked by a lawmaker about plans for chip factories in the southwest Both Samsung and SK Hynix said they would now be able to speed up ‌construction in Yongin as the government had pledged to accelerate approvals, as part of efforts to meet surging demand During Monday's presentation attended by President Lee, SK Hynix's Chey said the company would monitor demand conditions as it determined the scale of investment but added that currently it was still difficult to fully address the supply shortage. 이 기사는 해당 기업과 연결된 해외 보도를 바탕으로 정리했다.
